title: Construction Breaks Ground At 105 West 108th Street on Manhattan’s Upper West Side
summary: Last week, construction broke ground on 105 West 108th Street, a forthcoming 13-story affordable housing building on Manhattan's Upper West Side. Designed by Dattner Architects and developed by The West Side Federation for Senior and Supportive Housing, the project will yield 84 permanent senior housing units for adults 62 years and older, with 40 units reserved for adults 55 and over who have experienced homelessness and are living with serious mental illness and/or substance use disorder. The upcoming structure is part of phase two of the WSFSSH campus, and is being built on an interior lot by the corner of West 108th Street and Columbus Avenue.
author: Michael Young

title: Below-Grade Work Progresses At 265 Wyckoff Street in Boerum Hill, Brooklyn
summary: Earthwork is unfolding at 265 Wyckoff Street, the site of an 11-story residential building in Boerum Hill, Brooklyn. Designed by Fischer Rasmussen Whitfield Architects and developed by Yitzchook Katz of Goose Property Management, the structure will yield 70 units. The property is located between 3rd Avenue and Nevins Street.
author: Michael Young and Matt Pruznick

title: Agreement Reached To Preserve Rent Protection At Gateway Plaza In Battery Park City, Manhattan
summary: Battery Park City Authority and local elected officials have reached an agreement with LeFrak and its partners, the Olnick Organization and Fisher Development, doing business as Marina Towers Associates, to preserve rent protections for about 430 units at Gateway Plaza in Battery Park City, Manhattan. The deal extends protections that had been set to expire in July 2030 through June 17, 2069, covering residents who have remained in continuous occupancy since July 1, 2009 and maintain their primary residence in New York State.
author: Max Gillespie

title: Permits Filed for 4541 Furman Avenue in Wakefield, The Bronx
summary: Permits have been filed for a 12-story mixed-use building at 4541 Furman Avenue in Wakefield, The Bronx. Located between East 239th Street and East 240th Street, the lot is near the Wakefield-241st Street subway station, served by the 2 train. Tom Eswein of Vertical Community Development is listed as the owner behind the applications.
author: Vanessa Londono
